channel.title: Insights Hub - Cox Automotive Inc.
channel.description: Transforming the Way the World Buys, Sells, Owns and Uses Cars.

item.title: Kelley Blue Book Report: As Buyers Gravitate Toward More Affordable Segments, Industry-Wide Vehicle Prices Hold Steady in June
item.dc:creator: Sam Blum
item.description:
New-vehicle prices increased modestly in June, according to data released today by Kelley Blue Book, a Cox Automotive brand. The average transaction price (ATP) for a new vehicle purchased in June increased less than 1% year over year to $49,758,…

The post Kelley Blue Book Report: As Buyers Gravitate Toward More Affordable Segments, Industry-Wide Vehicle Prices Hold Steady in June appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Auto Market Weekly Summary
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Robb
item.description:
This edition of the Auto Market Weekly Summary includes updates on new-vehicle pricing, auto credit availability, consumer credit, and the minutes from the June Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting. The conflict in the Middle East intensified this past week…

The post Auto Market Weekly Summary appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: EV Market Stabilizes in Q2, as New Entries Help Slow Sharp Sales Decline
item.dc:creator: Lauren.Hensler@coxautoinc.com
item.description:
The electrified vehicle story in the first half of 2026 is increasingly a story about hybrids. While the overall U.S. new-vehicle market is expected to decline by 2.2% compared to a year ago, according to Kelley Blue Book counts, hybrid sales are…

The post EV Market Stabilizes in Q2, as New Entries Help Slow Sharp Sales Decline appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Credit Availability Index Hits 10-year High as Lenders, Consumers Take on More Risk
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Gregory
item.description:
In June 2026, the Dealertrack Credit Availability Index rose to 104.6, its highest level in more than a decade. The All-Loans Index increased 0.9% from May’s 103.6, marking its fifth consecutive monthly increase, and a rise of 7.3% from June…

The post Credit Availability Index Hits 10-year High as Lenders, Consumers Take on More Risk appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Replay Available: Q2 2026 Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index Call
item.dc:creator: Lauren.Hensler@coxautoinc.com
item.description:
A replay of the Q2 2026 Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index Call held on Wednesday, July 8, is available. Cox Automotive Chief Economist Jeremy Robb, Jonathan Gregory, senior director of Economic and Industry Insights, and Scott Vanner, manager of Economic and Industry Insights, reported the latest finding…

The post Replay Available: Q2 2026 Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index Call appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index Normalizes in Q2 After Strong Start in 2026; Retail Used-Vehicle Prices Climb Higher
item.dc:creator: Lauren.Hensler@coxautoinc.com
item.description:
ATLANTA, July 8, 2026 – Wholesale used-vehicle prices (on a mix-, mileage- and seasonally adjusted basis) were higher year over year in June as the used-vehicle market closed the first half of 2026 with healthy demand and stable pricing conditions.…

The post Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index Normalizes in Q2 After Strong Start in 2026; Retail Used-Vehicle Prices Climb Higher appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index: June 2026 Trends
item.dc:creator: Lauren.Hensler@coxautoinc.com
item.description:
Key Metrics Expert Perspective — Jonathan Gregory, Senior Director, Cox Automotive “The first half of the year is officially in the books, and wholesale values finished on solid footing. The Manheim Index closed June up 2.1% against last year and…

The post Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index: June 2026 Trends appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Put Inventory Strategy First: How Independent Dealers Can Compete When Supply Is Tight
item.dc:creator: burke
item.description:
Independent dealers don’t need a report to tell them the market is tight, they’re living it every day. Inventory is harder to find, more expensive to acquire, and increasingly contested by larger dealers with more scale, capital and access to…

The post Put Inventory Strategy First: How Independent Dealers Can Compete When Supply Is Tight appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Auto Market Weekly Summary
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Robb
item.description:
As holiday celebrations were getting underway last week, we received updates on new automotive sales, jobs and unemployment, consumer confidence, and home prices—all included below in this Auto Market Weekly Summary. Gas prices continue to decline, but now at a…

item.title: Bonus Episode: The EV Head Fake: How Regulatory Push and Wasted Capital Transform the Industry

item.title: Vehicle Affordability in America: The Car Is Not the Villain
item.dc:creator: Erin Keating
item.description:
The headline is easy to write. The average new vehicle transacts consistently at or around $50,000. It is also easy to understand why that number gets so much attention. The point came through clearly in Cox Automotive’s 2026 Mid-Year Review…

The post Vehicle Affordability in America: The Car Is Not the Villain appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Cox Automotive Forecast: New-Vehicle Sales Pace Holds Strong Through First Half of 2026 Despite Continued Economic and Policy Uncertainty
item.dc:creator: Lauren.Hensler@coxautoinc.com
item.description:
Updated, July 2, 2026: Despite persistent economic headwinds, the U.S. new-vehicle market delivered a stronger-than-expected result in June. According to preliminary estimates, June sales likely reached 1.36 million units, up 7.2% year over year. The seasonally adjusted annual rate (SAAR) of…

The post Cox Automotive Forecast: New-Vehicle Sales Pace Holds Strong Through First Half of 2026 Despite Continued Economic and Policy Uncertainty appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Now Available: Cox Automotive 2026 Mid-Year Review

item.description:
The Cox Automotive Q2 2026 Mid-Year Review is now available, offering a comprehensive look at how the U.S. auto market is performing through the first half of the year. The Cox Automotive Economic and Industry Insights team breaks down what’s really driving today’s market…

The post Now Available: Cox Automotive 2026 Mid-Year Review appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: 2026 Cox Automotive Forecasts
item.dc:creator: Dara Hailes
item.description:
Cox Automotive provides forecasts on the new and used vehicle markets, leasing, fleet and CPO sales, and the Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index. Updates (highlighted) to the full-year 2026 forecasts were announced during the Cox Automotive Q2 2026 Mid-Year Review on June 24,…

item.title: Cox Automotive Awards $10,000 NIADA Foundation Scholarship to Northwood University Student Mallorie Hollenbeck
item.dc:creator: Sam Blum
item.description:
ATLANTA, June 23, 2026 – Cox Automotive today announced that Mallorie Hollenbeck, a student at Northwood University in Midland, Michigan, has been named the 2026 recipient of the $10,000 National Independent Automobile Dealers Association (NIADA) Foundation Northwood University Scholarship, presented…

The post Cox Automotive Awards $10,000 NIADA Foundation Scholarship to Northwood University Student Mallorie Hollenbeck appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: New Cox Automotive Research Reveals Independent Dealership Buyers Are Driven by Necessity—Not Aspiration
item.dc:creator: Lauren.Hensler@coxautoinc.com
item.description:

item.dc:creator: Lauren.Hensler@coxautoinc.com

Key Metrics Expert Perspective — Jeremy Robb, Chief Economist, Cox Automotive “Manheim values continue their normal pattern of declining at this time of year, though prices are falling from elevated levels from stronger spring appreciation. The increase in the first…

item.title: Price Drop and Income Growth Improve New-Vehicle Affordability in May
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Gregory
item.description:
New-vehicle affordability improved in May, as lower transaction prices, higher incentives, and strong income growth more than offset a slight increase in interest rates. The estimated average new-vehicle auto loan rate increased by 8 basis points to 9.53%, which was…

The post Price Drop and Income Growth Improve New-Vehicle Affordability in May appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: EV Market Monitor – May 2026
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Valdez Streaty
item.description:
The EV market showed further signs of stabilization in May, following softer conditions in April. While new EV sales improved sequentially, overall demand remains below year-ago levels. The annual decline of 21.9%, however, was the smallest since government support was…

The post EV Market Monitor – May 2026 appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Used Vehicle Sales Pace Slows in May as Prices Climb Higher
item.dc:creator: Scott Vanner
item.description:
According to Cox Automotive’s analysis of vAuto Live Market View data, used-vehicle inventory increased in May as the retail sales pace slowed. Average listing prices rose for the third consecutive month, reaching $26,918, the highest since mid-2023 and up 6%…

The post Used Vehicle Sales Pace Slows in May as Prices Climb Higher appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: May New-Vehicle Inventory Holds Near 2026 Norms, But a Stronger Sales Pace Tightened Days’ Supply Across Most Brands
item.dc:creator: Erin Keating
item.description:
May’s new-vehicle market was defined by a clear three-part story: Inventory stayed essentially steady, sales accelerated, and days’ supply tightened as a result. vAuto Live Market View showed 2.89 million units in inventory in May, up modestly from April and…

The post May New-Vehicle Inventory Holds Near 2026 Norms, But a Stronger Sales Pace Tightened Days’ Supply Across Most Brands appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Auto Credit Availability Climbs in May as Approval Rates Improve, Yield Spreads Narrow
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Gregory
item.description:
In May 2026, the Dealertrack Credit Availability Index rose to 103.7, its highest level since April 2022. The All-Loans Index increased 1.5% from April’s 102.2 and is up about 7.3% from May 2025. While the index posted one of its…

The post Auto Credit Availability Climbs in May as Approval Rates Improve, Yield Spreads Narrow appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.dc:creator: Lauren.Hensler@coxautoinc.com
item.description:
ATLANTA, June 9, 2026 – New-vehicle prices moderated slightly in May and incentive spending grew, according to data released today by Kelley Blue Book, a Cox Automotive brand. The average transaction price (ATP) for a new vehicle purchased in May declined…

item.description:

item.dc:creator: Lauren.Hensler@coxautoinc.com
item.description:
Updated, June 4, 2026: Initial indications suggest the new-vehicle sales pace in May was near 16.1 million, aligned with the Cox Automotive forecast, as outlined below. The sales pace in May was higher year over year for the first time in 2026.…

item.title: Cox Automotive Report: Dealer Sentiment Improves on Current Conditions in Q2, Outlook Weakens for Months Ahead
item.dc:creator: Lauren.Hensler@coxautoinc.com
item.description:
ATLANTA, May 26, 2026 – The Q2 2026 Cox Automotive Dealer Sentiment Index (CADSI) shows a second consecutive quarterly gain in dealer current market sentiment, driven by stronger conditions following a healthy spring selling season. However, expectations for the months…

item.title: New-Vehicle Affordability Declines in April on Higher Prices, Lower Incentives
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Gregory
item.description:
New-vehicle affordability declined slightly in April, as higher prices, rising interest rates, and lower incentives outweighed strong income growth. The estimated average auto loan rate increased by 9 basis points to 9.45% in April, while the Kelley Blue Book average…

The post New-Vehicle Affordability Declines in April on Higher Prices, Lower Incentives appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Auto Market Weekly Summary
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Robb
item.description:
This edition of the Auto Market Weekly Summary includes updates on credit availability, consumer price inflation, producer price inflation, and retail sales, which held up in April better than the headlines suggest. Core spending last month showed resilience, even as…

The post Auto Market Weekly Summary appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Used-Vehicle Inventory Recovers from Record Low Levels, Increases in April as Sales Moderate
item.dc:creator: Scott Vanner
item.description:
According to Cox Automotive’s analysis of vAuto Live Market View data, used-vehicle inventory increased in April from March’s record low, though levels remained lower year over year. As the tax-refund season eased from its peak, the sales pace moderated and…

The post Used-Vehicle Inventory Recovers from Record Low Levels, Increases in April as Sales Moderate appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: EV Market Monitor – April 2026
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Valdez Streaty
item.description:
The electric vehicle market softened in April following March’s rebound, with performance diverging between the new and used segments. New EV sales declined amid broader weakness in overall vehicle demand, even as elevated fuel prices renewed consumer focus on efficiency.…

The post EV Market Monitor – April 2026 appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Ep. 8: Pricing Pressures and EV Momentum in a Tight Used Market
item.dc:creator: burke
item.description:
The Auto Market Brief – Ep. #8 In this Episode Pricing pressures are building across the auto market while inflation remains elevated, labor signals are mixed, and demand continues to adjust rather than retreat. In this episode of The Auto Market…

The post Ep. 8: Pricing Pressures and EV Momentum in a Tight Used Market appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: New-Vehicle Inventory Holds Steady in April as Automakers Sell Down Model Year 2025 Inventory
item.dc:creator: Erin Keating
item.description:
The U.S. new-vehicle market entered May with mostly stable inventory levels and a modest decline in overall volume as automakers deliberately rebalance inventory by clearing out MY2025 vehicles. Industry days’ supply, as measured by vAuto Live Market View, declined 2.4%…

The post New-Vehicle Inventory Holds Steady in April as Automakers Sell Down Model Year 2025 Inventory appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..

item.title: Auto Credit Availability Inches Higher in April, Despite Pullback in Subprime Lending
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Gregory
item.description:
In April 2026, the Dealertrack Credit Availability Index rose to 102.4, its highest level since June 2022, though essentially unchanged from March’s revised-lower 102.3. The All-Loans Index increased just 0.1% from March and is up approximately 7.2% from April 2025.…

The post Auto Credit Availability Inches Higher in April, Despite Pullback in Subprime Lending appeared first on Cox Automotive Inc..
